calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2024 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Continued To Mar-25-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Nov-09-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Victoria Knowles, Esq. 949-706-6464 Pacific Trial Attorneys for Plaintiff.
Philip Stillman, Esq. 888-235-4279 Stillman & Associates for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms. Knowles argues. Mr. Stillman agrees with the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LUIS LICEA MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication is denied. Plaintiff failed to maintain its initial burden of production. (CCP 437c(p)(1).)

Plaintiff correctly notes that he need not establish actual damages to recover statutory damages under Civil Code sec. 52(a). (See Angelucci v. Cent. Supper Club (2007) 41 Cal.4th 160, 175 [explaining Unruh Act standing]; Molski v. M.J. Cable, Inc., 481 F.3d 724, 731 (9th Cir. 2007)[?The litigant need not prove she suffered actual damages to recover the independent statutory damages of $4,000.?]; Botosan v. Paul McNally Realty, 216 F.3d 827, 835 (9th Cir. 2000) [[P]roof of actual damages is not a prerequisite to recovery of statutory minimum damages.].)

Plaintiff argues that [a] prevailing plaintiff is entitled to minimum statutory damages of $4,000 for each time he was discriminated against, and each time he was deterred based on that discrimination. (Plaintiff's points and authorities, 22:23-24 (emphasis in original).) Plaintiff further states that he is entitled to a "minimum of $8,000 in statutory damages." (Id. at 23:7-8 (emphasis in original).)

Plaintiff's separate statement of undisputed facts does not sufficiently establish the damages request. Plaintiff apparently seeks $4,000 for the time he attempted to access defendant's website on March 13, 2020. (UMF 7.) He also apparently seeks $4,000 because the website continues to pose accessibility barriers. (UMF 17.) Plaintiff, however, cannot recover damages for general accessibility deterrence. Rather, plaintiff must show that he was deterred on a particular occasion. (Civil Code sec. 52(a) [liable for each offense], Civil Code sec. 55.56(a), (b) [statutory damages for violation of Civil Code 52 "on a particular occasion"]; Shaw v. Kelley 2019 WL 497620 at *10.) Here, plaintiff claims that he continues to be deterred, but does not cite a particular occasion. (UMF 17.) Plaintiff therefore fails to show that he is entitled to $8,000.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
